plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy XLV to diversify into a new, lower-beta sector.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and low drawdown.
A significant cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month return, emphasizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and diversifying, leading to lower potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to adhere to concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged exposure and exit losing positions.
Trim QQQI as flagged by the scanner, rebalancing the portfolio.
Buy GOOGL to add a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner read.
Add RTX for diversification into a different sector with good risk/reward.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month growth, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while introducing quality tech, balancing upside with controlled drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits, freeing capital for higher-conviction plays.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit losing positions and reallocate capital into stronger setups.
Trim QQQI as flagged, aligning with an aggressive growth posture.
Buy CRDO and ANET, leveraging their high scanner scores and strong risk/reward.
Increase SMH exposure, leaning into the strong uptrend in semiconductors.
This plan aggressively targets growth, concentrating in high-potential setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage. Be aware that this approach significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay, leading to potentially large and rapid losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and free capital for income plays.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta names (SMH, CRWD) to reduce risk.
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize income-generating exposure.
Reduce QQQ exposure, shifting focus from growth to steady distributions.
This plan prioritizes reliable monthly distributions and low drawdown.
A cash buffer will be maintained for stability and opportunistic entry.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, meaning it's not a savings account and capital is still at risk.
